useWhen: Stacking content vertically with consistent spacing
Building form layouts
Composing a screen body with multiple sections
avoidWhen: Content is scrollable — wrap in scroll instead
Horizontal layout is needed — use hstack

useWhen: Placing elements side by side in a row
Building toolbar-like rows with a spacer
Label + value pairs on the same line
avoidWhen: Content needs to wrap to multiple lines
Vertical layout is needed — use vstack

useWhen: Overlaying content on top of a background shape or image
Placing a badge or overlay on top of another node
avoidWhen: Nodes need explicit alignment with frame constraints — use frame
Vertical or horizontal flow layout is needed

useWhen: Content may exceed the visible screen area
Horizontal scrolling chip rows or card carousels
Any screen that is not a List
avoidWhen: Content is guaranteed to fit on screen
A native document list with sections is needed — use list
Wrapping a list node — list is already scrollable and scroll will collapse it to zero height

useWhen: Displaying a static or state-bound string
Rendering a formatted template with multiple bound values
avoidWhen: An icon is also needed alongside the text — use label
The text should be editable — use textfield

useWhen: A tappable action is required
Confirming, submitting, or triggering any event
avoidWhen: A boolean on/off control is needed — use toggle
The tap should navigate — use nav.push action inside button

useWhen: A boolean on/off switch bound to a state variable
Settings rows with an enable/disable control
avoidWhen: Multiple exclusive options are needed — use a different pattern
The value is not boolean
toggle.changeEventNote: The binding writes the new boolean value to state before the change handler fires. Use the change event only for side effects on other keys (e.g. ui.toast, state.set on a different key). Never call state.toggle or state.set on the same bind key inside the change handler — it will immediately invert the value back, making the toggle appear broken. If no side effect is needed, omit the event field entirely.

useWhen: A standalone SF Symbol icon with explicit size is needed
Decorative icon with no accompanying text
avoidWhen: An icon paired with a text label is needed — use label
The icon is part of a button — use button with icon prop

useWhen: Pushing sibling elements to opposite ends of an hstack or vstack
Creating flexible empty space between nodes
avoidWhen: Fixed spacing between elements is needed — use the spacing prop on the parent stack

useWhen: Displaying a small highlighted count or tag
Status chips with a pill/capsule background
avoidWhen: Plain text display is needed — use text
Custom styling is required — badge has fixed inline styling

useWhen: Displaying an SF Symbol icon paired with a text title
Settings rows, menu items, or list rows with an icon
avoidWhen: No icon is needed — use text
The icon and text need independent styling — use hstack with icon and text nodes

useWhen: A dropdown menu with multiple contextual actions is needed
Toolbar or list row with an ellipsis/more button
avoidWhen: Only one action is available — use button
Actions should always be visible — use buttons in an hstack

useWhen: Top-level navigation between two or more major screens
App-level tab bar at the bottom of the screen
avoidWhen: Segmented in-screen switching — use state + visibleWhen pattern instead
Only one screen exists
tabview.note: tabview must be the root child of the screen document. Each tab that needs push navigation must contain its own navigationstack as the tab's root child — do NOT wrap the entire tabview in a navigationstack.

useWhen: As a direct child of tabview to define a tab's content and tab bar item
When the tab needs a navigation bar, place a navigationstack as the tab's first child
avoidWhen: Outside of a tabview — it renders as a plain vstack with no tab bar item

useWhen: Displaying an SF Symbol as a resizable image
Displaying an asset catalog image by name
avoidWhen: A small inline icon is needed — use icon or label
No actual image source is known — a placeholder will appear

useWhen: The screen needs a navigation bar with a title
Toolbar buttons in topLeading, topTrailing, principal, or bottomBar slots are needed
The screen is the root of a push-navigation flow
A tab inside a tabview needs its own navigation bar — place navigationstack as the tab's root child
avoidWhen: No navigation bar or toolbar is needed — use vstack or scroll
The screen is already inside a navigationstack
The screen root is a tabview — navigationstack must not wrap tabview; place it inside each tab instead
navigationstack.navPushNote: nav.push requires a screenId that corresponds to an actually loaded screen document in the runtime. Do not invent placeholder screenId values such as 'detail-screen' or 'profile-screen' unless those screens genuinely exist as loaded documents. If no second screen exists, do not use nav.push — use ui.toast or ui.alert as a placeholder action instead.

useWhen: A native document-style grouped or plain list with section support
Settings screens with multiple sections and rows
avoidWhen: Custom row spacing or background is needed — use vstack in scroll
Horizontal layout is needed
Wrapping inside a scroll node — list manages its own scrolling; wrapping it in scroll collapses it to zero height

useWhen: Grouping rows inside a list with an optional header title
avoidWhen: Outside of a list — use group instead for card or plain grouping

useWhen: Applying explicit alignment (e.g. bottomTrailing) to children within fixed dimensions
Clipping children to a specific size with clip or clipShape
avoidWhen: Simple overlay without alignment constraints — use zstack
No fixed dimensions are needed

useWhen: Single-line text input bound to a state variable
Search fields, name fields, email fields, password fields
avoidWhen: Multi-line input is needed — not currently supported
Value is not a string
textfield.changeEventNote: The binding writes the new string value to state on every keystroke, before the change handler fires. Use the change event only for side effects on other keys. Never call state.set on the same bind key inside the change handler.

useWhen: Continuous or stepped numeric value selection bound to a state variable
Volume controls, ratings, progress adjustments
avoidWhen: A discrete set of options is needed — use buttons or a different pattern
The value is not numeric
slider.changeEventNote: The binding writes the new numeric value to state before the change handler fires. Use the change event only for side effects on other keys or UI feedback (e.g. ui.toast). Never call state.set, state.inc, or state.dec on the same bind key inside the change handler.

useWhen: Drawing a geometric shape (circle, rectangle, capsule, ellipse, roundedRectangle)
Using a shape as a colored background block or decorative divider
Overlaying child nodes on top of a shape via its children array
avoidWhen: Background color on an existing node is sufficient — use backgroundColor common prop
Clipping a container to a shape — use clipShape common prop

useWhen: Grouping related content outside of a list with an optional title and footer
Collapsible sections with a disclosure toggle
Card-style or inset-style content blocks
avoidWhen: Content is inside a list — use section instead
No visual grouping is needed — use vstack

useWhen: Embedding a live web page or web-based content inside a native screen
Displaying content that already exists as a URL and does not need native reconstruction
Injecting JavaScript into an embedded page via eval actions
avoidWhen: A native node exists that achieves the same result — prefer native nodes
No id is provided — webview without id renders an error placeholder and cannot be targeted by eval
notes: id is required on every webview node — it registers the view in WebViewRegistry
eval is the only way to interact with a webview from the event system
eval goes through the normal event → named event → actions pipeline — no special wiring needed

useWhen: Injecting JavaScript into a webview node in response to a user action
Calling a JS function, changing DOM state, or scrolling inside a webview
avoidWhen: The target id does not match any webview node in the same document — the action is silently ignored
Native state changes are needed — use state.set / state.toggle instead
notes: target must be the exact id of a webview node declared in the same document
script is a raw JavaScript string — it is evaluated directly in the webview's JS context
